title: Appointment
description: A patient's existing / booked appointment at NYC Health + Hospitals — the read side of scheduling, as surfaced in the MyChart patient portal. This maps directly to the FHIR Appointment resource, which the live Epic FHIR R4 API DOES expose (read + search-type), but only under SMART patient authorization. Note the asymmetry the assessment turns on: the standard supports reading an Appointment, but the H+H FHIR surface exposes NO create/booking, NO Slot resource, and NO $book/$find operation — so booking has no machine-readable contract. See appointment-request.json for the net-new write object.

x-fhir.note: read-only on the H+H FHIR endpoint; no create, no Slot, no $book

serviceType.description: Reason / service type for the visit (FHIR Appointment.serviceType).

provider.description: The practitioner for the visit (FHIR Appointment.participant → Practitioner).
